source: ISO 11228-1:2021 Manual Handling + EN 547 Human Body Measurements + Neufert Architects Data 4th Ed + NIOSH Lifting Equation
topic: Industrial worker ergonomics — reach, posture, workstation heights, and fatigue zones

facts.context: 50th percentile standing worker height (male European reference — Neufert)
50th percentile shoulder height — upper limit for comfortable horizontal reach
50th percentile elbow height — optimal workbench height for precision tasks
Recommended workbench height for light assembly tasks — EN 547 / Neufert
Lower bench for heavy pushing/pulling tasks — reduces shoulder strain
Comfortable forward reach without leaning — ISO 11228-3
Maximum forward reach with slight lean — beyond this requires repositioning
Maximum overhead reach for occasional access — not suitable for repetitive tasks
Comfortable overhead reach without strain — upper limit for frequent access
Minimum comfortable reach height from floor without bending — repetitive access below this causes injury
Comfortable lateral reach to the side — controls and tools should be within this radius
Floor depth allocated for a standing operator at a workstation — from machine face
Minimum clear width for two operators working side by side at the same bench
OSHA — minimum passageway behind a standing operator at a fixed workstation — 36 in
NIOSH recommended max lift weight at optimal height (waist level, close to body)
NIOSH recommended max lift from floor level — twisting and distance reduce this further
Recommended max continuous standing work before a break — beyond causes lower limb fatigue
Anti-fatigue mat depth from machine face — covers standing operator zone
Optimal eye-to-part distance for precision visual inspection tasks — 30 cm
Maximum comfortable inspection distance without optical aids — parts should be within this range
Maximum height for frequently accessed tool storage — avoids overhead reach in repetitive tasks
Minimum height for tool storage to avoid repeated bending — 400mm from finished floor
